rulings.Case Title: ROBERT ROSE VS. NINA GURALNIK ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Dismiss Cross-Complaint; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 6, 2022 line 1. PLAINTIFF ROBERT ROSE Notice Of Motion To Dismiss Cross-Complaint is OFF CALENDAR, moot. Order dismissing Cros-Complaint entered on August 16, 2022. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: KEITH GOODMAN VS. AUDREY DER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 6, 2022 line 2. CROSS DEFENDANT ANDREW DER, JUNE KWAN, BEVERLY YOUNG, DAISY UNG Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Arbitration is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Request to compel arbitration is DENIED. Moving party failed to show that the cross-complaint for indemnity is "a dispute covered by the agreement." Furthermore, there is pending litigation with a third party with a potential for conflicting ruling on common issues of law and fact (habitability cross-complaint). CCP 1281.2(c). Moving party failed to cite any authority for the proposition that two parties may contract around this provision and failed to provide convincing argument that April 22, 2021 stipulation in fact contemplated third-party cross-complaint that has not been filed until September 15, 2021. However, April 22, 2021 stipulation does appear to indicate the parties' intention not to proceed with "any" litigation in San Francisco Superior Court until pending arbitration is resolved. Therefore, Cross-Complaint for indemnity is stayed until the completion of the pending Arbitration proceedings (assuming they have not been completed as of the date of this order). =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: KENNY LU VS. DUC T. LUU ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 6, 2022 line 6. DEFENDANT DUC LUU, CHAU NGUYEN MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS is DENIED. Moving party failed to overcome the presumption afforded to the registered process servers.
